title: Today's AI Brief: Kevin O’Leary agrees to downsize massive Utah data center
summary: A look at the latest AI news, including Kevin O'Leary's data center downsizing and the AI IPO race.

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to today's AI Brief. I've got a few interesting stories to share with you from the world of artificial intelligence. First up, Kevin O'Leary, also known as 'Mr. Wonderful' from Shark Tank, has agreed to downsize his massive Utah data center project, known as The Stratos Project. Now, I know what you're thinking - what's the big deal about a data center? Well, this one's footprint is still going to be larger than Manhattan, which is just mind-boggling.

According to The Verge, O'Leary's project will still be huge, but it's a step in the right direction for those concerned about the environmental impact of these massive data centers. I mean, we're talking about a lot of energy consumption here. On a lighter note, I guess you could say O'Leary is taking a 'scaled-down' approach. This move comes as the AI industry is experiencing a surge in growth, and environmental concerns are becoming more pressing.

Next, Wired reports that the AI IPO race is heating up, with several top AI companies looking to go public. This is interesting because it shows just how much interest there is in AI right now. My read is that we're going to see a lot of movement in this space over the next year or so. It's exciting to think about what this could mean for the future of AI development.

My quick read: the artificial intelligence story keeps getting bigger, but also stranger in very human ways. One moment it is strategy and infrastructure; the next, the Vatican is in the room. That is a pretty good sign this technology has left the lab and wandered into the town square.
